faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How does the AI face analyzer work?
faq.a1: The AI detects 478 facial landmarks using MediaPipe Face Landmarker, running entirely in your browser. It calculates geometric proportions — symmetry, golden ratio alignment, facial thirds balance — and converts these measurements into human-readable scores. No photo is ever uploaded to any server.
faq.q2: Is my photo uploaded to a server?
faq.a2: No. All processing happens locally in your browser. Your photo stays on your device and is destroyed when you close or refresh the page. Our code is open source (Apache 2.0) — anyone can audit this claim.
faq.q3: How is the face score calculated?
faq.a3: We measure 7 facial dimensions: bilateral symmetry (20%), golden ratio alignment (20%), facial thirds balance (15%), five-eye alignment (15%), eye spacing ratio (10%), nose-lip proportion (10%), and jaw contour smoothness (10%). Each dimension is scored independently, then weighted into a final score mapped to a 60–99 range.
faq.q4: What is facial symmetry and why does it matter?
faq.a4: Facial symmetry measures how closely the left and right sides of your face mirror each other. It's one of the most studied features in facial aesthetics research. While perfect symmetry is rare and not necessarily 'ideal,' higher symmetry scores are generally associated with facial harmony across cultures.
faq.q5: How accurate is the AI face attractiveness test?
faq.a5: This is not an attractiveness test — it's a geometric analysis. The score reflects mathematical proportions, not subjective beauty. Results should be taken as a fun reference rather than a definitive assessment. Real attractiveness involves personality, expression, and cultural context that no algorithm can measure.
faq.q6: What's the difference between face score and real attractiveness?
faq.a6: Face score measures geometric ratios against classical ideals (golden ratio, symmetry). Real attractiveness is subjective — it involves facial expressiveness, cultural preferences, personality, and individual taste. Our score provides a mathematical reference point, not a judgment.
